# 04 — Chosen Direction: Mode 2 + Hosted LLM Interpretation

This file records a **scoping decision** made with the user. It narrows the broad
goal in `03-ultimate-goal-jetson.md` to the concrete thing to build first.

## Decision in one paragraph

Build on **Mode 2** (edge feature extraction). Keep the existing **model-free**
detection exactly as-is — it produces the flags `FALL? / person-active / quiet`
with no model and no network. Add an **interpretation layer** on top: an
**expanded, per-event caregiver note** generated by a **hosted (cloud) LLM**,
designed to **degrade gracefully** because connectivity is intermittent. Mode 1
(raw A/V streaming) is set aside for the Jetson — not because it lacks code (it
has `mode1_streamer.py` + the relay's `/ingest_raw`), but because raw streaming is
heavy on bandwidth and CPU and a poor fit for a small edge device.

## Confirmed constraints (from the user)

| Question | Answer | Consequence |
|---|---|---|
| Which board? | **Original Jetson Nano 4GB** (2019, Maxwell, JetPack 4.6, Ubuntu 18.04) — *not* the Orin Nano | **A local LLM is effectively infeasible.** Interpretation must be a **remote hosted API**. |
| Internet at runtime? | **Sometimes / hybrid** | LLM enrichment must be **optional and resilient** — the system must stay useful offline. |
| LLM's job? | **Expanded per-event note** (richer/configurable version of today's caregiver note) | Smallest sensible scope; closest to the existing `_maybe_llm_note`. **No temporal/aggregate reasoning for now.** |

> Device note: the user initially typed "Orin Nano" but confirmed it is the
> **original Jetson Nano 4GB**. Do not re-introduce the Orin assumption — it would
> wrongly imply local SLM inference is on the table.

## What already exists (you are expanding, not inventing)

`relay_server.py::_maybe_llm_note` (lines ~99–117) already:
- Fires **only** when `fall_suspected` is true (saves tokens; only real events).
- Returns `None` when there's no API key or no fall — i.e. it **already degrades
  gracefully** to "no note."
- Calls an Anthropic model (`LLM_MODEL`, default `claude-sonnet-5`) with a caregiver
  prompt and returns one sentence.
- Keeps the key **server-side**; clients never receive it.

The "real work" is therefore: **(a) swap/abstract the provider** to a hosted
option that fits (free tier, offline-tolerant), and **(b) enrich what the note
says and how it's configured** — while preserving the graceful-degradation and
key-safety properties already in the code.

## Hosted LLM options (all cloud — local is ruled out on this board)

| Option | Notes | Trade-off |
|---|---|---|
| **NVIDIA API Catalog / NIM** (build.nvidia.com, `integrate.api.nvidia.com`) | Free tier, **OpenAI-compatible** REST, hosts many open models (Llama, etc.). The "NVIDIA free LLM" the user asked about. | Free-tier rate/credit limits; needs a free NVIDIA account + key; needs internet. |
| **Other free-tier APIs** (e.g. Groq, Google Gemini) | OpenAI-/REST-compatible free tiers, fast. | Rate limits; ToS/privacy of sending event context to a third party; needs internet. |
| **Anthropic** (already wired) | Zero new integration work; highest quality. | Paid; needs internet + key. |

**Common to all**: they only work **online**. Given hybrid connectivity, the
design must treat the note as best-effort enrichment, never a hard dependency.

**Privacy stays intact regardless**: Mode 2 sends only features/labels, never
faces or voices — so even a third-party hosted LLM only ever sees
de-identified data ("loud sound + motion stopped"), not raw A/V.

## Graceful-degradation design (for hybrid connectivity)

Two layers, clearly separated:

1. **Always-on, offline path (no LLM):** feature extraction → rule-based flag
   (`FALL? / person-active / quiet`). Pure Python, no network. This must never
   depend on the LLM.
2. **Best-effort enrichment (LLM):** when online and the event is a fall, call the
   hosted LLM for the caregiver note. When offline:
   - **Simplest (matches current code):** skip the note (return `None`), keep the
     flag. Nothing is lost operationally.
   - **Nicer (fits store-and-forward):** buffer fall events and **back-fill** their
     notes when connectivity returns — reusing the same outbox idea Mode 2 already
     uses for features.

For the confirmed "expanded per-event note" scope, the simplest path (skip when
offline) is enough to start; back-fill is an optional enhancement.

## Status (resolved/updated later in the session)

- **Topology — RESOLVED (see `06`)**: **Role A** — Jetson = edge, **laptop =
  relay/cloud**. The LLM call runs on the laptop-relay and the **API key stays on the
  laptop, off the Jetson** — preserving the boss's "key never on the edge device"
  property.
- **Provider — leading candidate is Claude (see `05`)**, not NVIDIA. Claude is
  already wired (`_maybe_llm_note`, default `claude-sonnet-5`) and vision-capable.
  **Pending**: does the lab have Anthropic **Console** API access (separate from the
  Claude *Team* subscription)? If yes → Claude; if no → NVIDIA API Catalog free tier.
- **Still open — note content/config**: what the expanded note should include
  (severity, suggested action, `context` field usage) — to be specified before coding.

## Suggested next step

Move from concept to a concrete design: pick the provider (likely NVIDIA API
Catalog free tier), define the provider abstraction so the note layer is
model-agnostic, specify the "expanded note" contents, and decide the topology
(which fixes key placement). Then implement against `relay_server.py`'s existing
`_maybe_llm_note` seam.
